title: trema glomeratum
intro: Trema glomeratum, also known as the Indian lead tree or jangli pili, is a deciduous tree species native to tropical Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Trema glomeratum include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of the tree are used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
